mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in greater than 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with t-stat below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions upward and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or 60-day forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of co-expansion periods over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during periods of stable inflation reflects robust manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, triggering short-term momentum buying in equities over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 5-day forward returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5%, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly expand aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day SPX returns across multiple business cycles, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation acceleration above 0.4% monthly forces central banks to anticipate tighter monetary policy, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based economic expansion, which sustains corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and consumption growth signal produces negative 60-day returns in greater than 50% of quarters, or interaction term coefficients are statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals weakening manufacturing demand and rising inventory burdens, which triggers short-term equity sell-offs over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52% over a 4-year rolling window.
